nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: Cinnamon becard
nodes.summary: Cinnamon becard（Cinnamon becard，学名 Pachyramphus cinnamomeus），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点。

nodes.name: 常綠森林
nodes.summary: 常綠森林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联442种鸟类，包括紫冠雉、角冠雉、Razor-billed curassow等442种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 喬治·紐博爾德·勞倫斯
nodes.summary: 喬治·紐博爾德·勞倫斯是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联13种鸟类，包括Antillean nighthawk、Cinnamon becard、Black-tailed myiobius等13种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 次生林
nodes.summary: 次生林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联749种鸟类，包括孤䳍、Sickle-winged guan、黑額鳴冠雉等749种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 阿雷納爾火山
nodes.summary: 阿雷納爾火山是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Cinnamon becard，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The cinnamon becard (Pachyramphus cinnamomeus) is a passerine bird in the family Tityridae, the tityras, becards, and allies. It is found in Mexico, in every Central American country except El Salvador, and in Colombia, Ecuador, and Venezuela.{{cite book | last = | first = | title =Check-list of North American Birds | publisher =American Ornithologists' Union |edition =7th | date =1998 | location =Washington, D.C

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The cinnamon becard (Pachyramphus cinnamomeus) is a passerine bird in the family Tityridae, the tityras, becards, and allies. It is found in Mexico, in every Central American country except El Salvador, and in Colombia, Ecuador, and Venezuela.{{cite book | last = | first = | title =Check-list of North American Birds | publisher =American Ornithologists' Union |edition =7th | date =1998 | location =Washington, D.C

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: | title =Birds of Venezuela | publisher =Princeton University Press | edition =second | date =2003 | location =Princeton NJ | pages =646 | language =English }} The cinnamon becard inhabits the interior and edges of evergreen forest and secondary forest in the tropical and lower subtropical zones. It also occurs in somewhat open woodlands, riparian areas, clearings with tall trees, and coastally in mangroves. In elevation it ranges overall from sea level mostly to . It reaches in northern Central America, in Costa Rica, in Colombia, in Ecuador, and in Venezuela
